# GSR Email Templates — Canonical Library

*Cross-referenced from 879 AI conversation summaries + 2,438 actual sent emails (June 2024 – April 2026). Do not edit the locked style rules without an actual archive correction to justify it.*

---

## Style Rules (Non-Negotiable)

1. **No em-dashes.** They are an AI writing tell and Daniel flags them every time.
2. **No "I hope this email finds you well" or any stiff opener.** Cut it.
3. **No fact-dump openers.** Hook or ask first, context second.
4. **Salutation scales with relationship:**
   - First contact or formal: `Hello Dr. [LAST NAME],` or `Hello [Mr./Ms. LAST NAME],`
   - Established/returning: `Hey [FIRST NAME],` or `Hey Dr. [LAST NAME],`
5. **Sign as Daniel Allen, Producer, Genesis Science Network.** Never as David Rives.
6. **No specific partner station names.** Say "stations across the country" or just list the streaming platforms.
7. **Target length:** 75–150 words for lifecycle emails. Outreach emails can run to ~200 words max.
8. **Returning guests:** Drop the intro paragraph entirely. Go straight to the ask.
9. **Follow-up replies:** Acknowledge only what's new. Never restate already-communicated information.
10. **Interview direction:** Sound like a curious producer, not a scientist. Open-ended, conversational.

---

## Template 1: First Outreach (New Guest)

**Subject:** Remote Zoom Interview on The Genesis Science Report

```
Hello [Dr./Mr./Ms. LAST NAME],

This is Daniel Allen, the producer for Genesis Science Report, a science and
news show produced by David Rives Ministries. I'm reaching out because we would
love to have you on "GSR" for a remote Zoom interview to hear your insights on
the following news piece:

[ARTICLE TITLE / URL]

[1-2 sentences on the interview angle, tied to the guest's specific expertise.
Sound like a curious producer, not a scientist. Use future tense: "This segment
would look at..." Don't summarize the article — say what you want to explore.]

The interview is a short Zoom session, typically 8–13 minutes. We're looking to
record on [FILMING DATE]. I'll send over a guest packet once you confirm interest.
A phone number is helpful for day-of coordination.

Best,
Daniel Allen
Producer, Genesis Science Network
(615) 939-1773
dallen@davidrives.com
```

**Variable fields:** [Dr./Mr./Ms. LAST NAME], [ARTICLE TITLE / URL], [ANGLE — expertise-specific], [FILMING DATE]

**Notes:**
- For a 48-hour response ask, add: "We'd love to have you on, and I'll reach out within 48 hours of hearing back."
- Third-touch non-responder: cut to two sentences — article link + ask if they'd be open to it.
- Credibility signal (if cold contact): can mention Seth Dillon or NASA guests naturally in the angle sentence, not as a name-drop paragraph.

---

## Template 2: Returning Guest Outreach

**Subject:** [TOPIC AREA] — Another GSR Episode?

```
Hey [DR./FIRST NAME],

Came across this piece and thought it would be a great fit for you:

[ARTICLE TITLE / URL]

[1 sentence on why it fits their expertise, different from previous coverage.
Cross-reference prior topics before sending — don't retread covered ground.]

We're filming on [DATE]. Interested?

Best,
Daniel
```

**Variable fields:** [DR./FIRST NAME], [ARTICLE TITLE / URL], [ANGLE NOTE], [DATE]

**Notes:** Skip everything else. No intro, no show credentials, no logistics paragraph. They know the show.

---

## Template 3: Zoom Link (Day-Of)

**Subject:** Zoom Link — [DATE] at [TIME] CST

```
Hey [DR./FIRST NAME],

Looking forward to having you on the show today! Here is the Zoom meeting link:

[ZOOM URL]

Please join at [JOIN TIME - 15 MIN EARLY] so we have time to work through any
tech issues before we kick off.

Best,
Daniel Allen
Producer, Genesis Science Network
(615) 939-1773
dallen@davidrives.com
```

**Variable fields:** [DR./FIRST NAME], [DATE], [TIME], [ZOOM URL], [JOIN TIME]

**Notes:**
- If link changes, resend just the new link with a one-liner: "Here is the updated Zoom link: [URL]"
- Never say "the host." David by name is fine.

---

## Template 4: Interview Confirmation (Pre-Shoot Reminder)

**Subject:** Interview Confirmation — Genesis Science Report ([DATE])

```
Dear [Dr./Mr./Ms. LAST NAME],

This is a reminder and confirmation for your upcoming interview on Genesis
Science Report.

Date & Time: [DATE] | [TIME] CST

Please join the Zoom call 15 minutes early ([EARLY JOIN TIME] CST) so we can
address any tech issues before we start.

To help us prepare, please send the following by [MATERIALS DEADLINE]:

- Talking Points — 8–10 brief bullet points you'd like to cover
- Bio — 3–4 sentences
- Lower-Third Title — how you'd like to appear on-screen
  (format: NAME | AFFILIATION | CREDENTIAL, ALL CAPS)
- City and State/Country you'll be joining from (for on-screen graphics)

Guest information packet is attached. Reach me at dallen@davidrives.com or
(615) 939-1773 if anything comes up.

Best,
Daniel Allen
Producer, Genesis Science Report
dallen@davidrives.com | (615) 939-1773
```

**Variable fields:** [Dr./Mr./Ms. LAST NAME], [DATE], [TIME], [EARLY JOIN TIME], [MATERIALS DEADLINE]

---

## Template 5: Pre-Air Notification (Night Before or Day Of)

**Subject:** Your Interview Airs [Tomorrow Night / Tonight] on GSR

```
Hey [DR./FIRST NAME],

Just a heads up — you have an interview airing [tomorrow night / tonight]
(Tuesday, [DATE]) at 8 PM CST.

[YOUTUBE LIVE STREAM LINK - if available that night]
[NETWORK BROADCAST LINK - genesissciencenetwork.com]

You can also watch on Roku and Amazon Fire TV by searching "Genesis Science
Network."

Thanks again for being part of the show. I'll follow up next week with the
YouTube link once the episode is posted.

Best,
Daniel Allen
Producer, Genesis Science Network
(615) 939-1773
dallen@davidrives.com
```

**Variable fields:** [DR./FIRST NAME], [tomorrow night / tonight], [DATE], [YOUTUBE LIVE URL], [NETWORK BROADCAST URL]

**Notes:**
- Do NOT include the static YouTube URL here — that's for the post-air email.
- For a very established/casual relationship: opener can be even shorter — "Hey Michael, you've got another interview airing tomorrow night at 8!"
- For a formal first-timer: swap "Just a heads up" for "I'm excited to let you know that your interview will air..."

---

## Template 6: Post-Air YouTube Link

**Subject:** Your Genesis Science Report Interview Is Now on YouTube

```
Hey [DR./FIRST NAME],

Just wanted to let you know your recent interview for GSR is now available on
YouTube[, and the episode has already garnered [VIEW COUNT] views]!

You can watch it here: [YOUTUBE URL WITH TIMESTAMP]

Best,
Daniel Allen
Producer, Genesis Science Network
(615) 939-1773
dallen@davidrives.com
```

**Variable fields:** [DR./FIRST NAME], [VIEW COUNT — include only if impressive], [YOUTUBE URL — include ?t=SECONDS timestamp if episode has multiple guests]

**Minimal version (third-touch or cold relationship):**

```
Hi [NAME],

Just wanted to pass along the YouTube link now that the episode is live:
[YOUTUBE URL]

Thanks again for being part of the show.

Best,
Daniel
```

---

## Template 7: Post-Shoot Follow-Up (Estimated Air Date)

**Subject:** Thanks for Being on GSR

```
Hey [FIRST NAME],

Thanks so much for coming on — really appreciate your time.

Your segment is currently slated to air around [ESTIMATED AIR DATE]. I'll give
you a heads-up a day or two before it goes live.

Hope to have you back again.

Best,
Daniel
```

**Variable fields:** [FIRST NAME], [ESTIMATED AIR DATE]

---

## What to Automate (Phase 1 Build Targets)

| Trigger | Template | Auto-fill fields | Manual review |
|---|---|---|---|
| Episode air date set | Pre-air notification (#5) | Guest name, air date, live URL | Relationship-level opener |
| Episode posted to YouTube | Post-air link (#6) | Guest name, YouTube URL + timestamp, view count | View count phrasing |
| Guest confirms filming slot | Zoom link (#3) | Guest name, Zoom URL, date, times | None — fully automatable |
| Filming date set | Confirmation (#4) | Guest name, date, time, deadline | None — fully automatable |

---

## Correction Moments (Why These Rules Exist)

These are real corrections Daniel made in AI sessions — preserved so the automation doesn't repeat the same mistakes.

1. **Em-dashes used despite explicit rule.** Flagged in 4+ separate sessions as an "AI writing tell."
2. **Too formal for a returning guest.** AI included a full intro paragraph for Dr. Tour — a returning guest who needed just the ask.
3. **Too technical/scientific tone.** The Dr. Fabich outreach and Dr. Brian Thomas angle both had to be rewritten to sound like "a curious producer" not a scientist.
4. **Over-engineered follow-ups.** AI drafted 8 versions when Daniel wanted one generic template. Rule: one draft unless asked otherwise.
5. **Stiff/robotic confirmation emails.** Initial drafts required rewrites for natural tone.
6. **Unnecessary self-introduction.** AI added background recitation in emails to known guests.
7. **Fact-dump openers.** AI led with scientific facts instead of the hook/ask.
8. **Encyclopedic story description.** The DNA drug delivery angle was written too formally and had to be corrected toward a "more casual, natural tone."
9. **"Too formal" was flagged explicitly** for the Gordon Wilson email; Daniel asked for "casual."
